mnemonics: Use F.I.R.E.D. to remember the Unified Process Phases: Inception, Elaboration, Construction, Transition. Think of a project being 'fired' through these phases.
keyPoints: The Unified Process is iterative and incremental, not a single waterfall pass.
It is use-case driven, meaning requirements and design are organized around how users will interact with the system.
It is architecture-centric, focusing on building a robust architectural core early in the project.
commonMistakes: Treating the phases (Inception, Elaboration, Construction, Transition) as distinct, one-time events rather than overlapping and iterative cycles.
Assuming the process is rigid and prescriptive; it is a framework meant to be tailored and adapted to a specific project's needs.
